get.summary:
GetNetworkPolicies returns all NetworkPolicies in a cluster, optionally
filtered by namespace or by the set of deployments they apply to.
get.description:
When deployment_query is provided, only policies that are selected by at
least one matching deployment are returned. YAML is populated for all
returned policies.

Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if deployment_query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id restricts results to policies in this cluster. Required.

parameters.name: deploymentQuery
parameters.description:
deployment_query is an optional StackRox search query; when set, only
policies that apply to at least one matching deployment are returned.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: namespace restricts results to policies in this Kubernetes namespace.

get.summary:
GetAllowedPeersFromCurrentPolicyForDeployment returns all peers
(deployments and external entities) that are currently permitted to
communicate with the given deployment based on active NetworkPolicies.
get.description:
Each peer entry includes the port, protocol, and direction (ingress/egress).
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the deployment is not found.

post.summary:
ApplyNetworkPolicyYamlForDeployment applies a network policy modification
to the cluster of the specified deployment and records an undo record keyed
by deployment ID.
post.description:
Behaves like ApplyNetworkPolicy but stores the undo record per deployment
rather than per cluster.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the deployment does not exist.
Returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if no active Sensor connection exists for the cluster.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is used to look up the target cluster and to key the undo record.

post.summary:
ApplyNetworkPolicy applies a NetworkPolicy modification to a cluster via
the connected Sensor and stores an undo record.
post.description:
The modification must be non-empty and all policies must have non-empty
namespaces. Changes to the stackrox and kube-system namespaces are
forbidden and will return INVALID_ARGUMENT. The caller must have write
access to all namespaces referenced in the modification.

Returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if no active Sensor connection exists for the cluster.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to apply the modification to. Required.

parameters.name: modification
parameters.description: modification describes the policies to create/update and the policies to delete.

get.summary:
GetDiffFlowsBetweenPolicyAndBaselineForDeployment compares the peers
currently allowed by active NetworkPolicies against those that would be
allowed by the baseline-generated NetworkPolicy for the deployment.
get.description:
Returns added (newly allowed), removed (no longer allowed), and reconciled
(present in both, with per-property diffs) peer groups.

get.summary:
GetNetworkGraph returns a policy-based network connectivity graph for a
cluster, showing which deployments are permitted to communicate based on
the active NetworkPolicies (not observed flows).
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist or is not visible.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if cluster_id is empty.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to generate the graph for. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query filters deployments shown as query-match nodes using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: includePorts
parameters.description: If set to true, include port-level information in the network policy graph.

parameters.name: scope.query
parameters.description: query filters the scope using StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production").

post.summary:
GetBaselineGeneratedNetworkPolicyForDeployment generates a NetworkPolicy
for a specific deployment based on its network baseline (the set of
connections marked as expected).
post.description:
The result is a NetworkPolicyModification ready to be previewed or applied.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if deployment_id is empty.
Requires View access to NetworkPolicy and DeploymentExtension.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is the ID of the deployment to generate the policy for. Required.

get.summary:
GenerateNetworkPolicies auto-generates Kubernetes NetworkPolicies for
deployments in a cluster based on observed network flows.
get.description:
The result is a NetworkPolicyModification containing YAML for the generated
policies and references to existing policies to delete (per delete_existing
mode). Flows are restricted to those after network_data_since if set.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if cluster_id is empty.
Requires View access to NetworkPolicy and NetworkGraph.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to generate policies for. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters the deployments whose observed flows are used for generation,
using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: deleteExisting
parameters.description:
delete_existing controls whether existing policies are deleted before applying
the generated ones. Defaults to NONE if UNKNOWN.

 - NONE: Do not delete any existing network policies.
 - GENERATED_ONLY: Delete any existing *auto-generated* network policies.
 - ALL: Delete all existing network policies in the respective namespace.

parameters.name: networkDataSince
parameters.description:
network_data_since restricts flows used for generation to those observed
after this timestamp.

parameters.name: includePorts
parameters.description:
include_ports controls whether per-port connection details are reflected in
the generated policy rules.

get.summary:
GetNetworkGraphEpoch returns the current epoch counter for the network
policy graph of a cluster.
get.description:
The epoch increments whenever the network policy state changes. Clients
can poll this lightweight endpoint to detect when a full graph refresh
is needed.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id identifies the cluster whose epoch to retrieve. Required.

post.summary:
SimulateNetworkGraph previews how the network connectivity graph would
change if the proposed NetworkPolicy modification were applied.
post.description:
Returns the simulated graph and the status of each affected policy.
When include_node_diff is true, also returns the sets of added and removed
connectivity edges.

Modifications to the stackrox or kube-system namespaces are rejected
unless the policy content is identical to the existing policy.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to simulate against. Required.

parameters.name: modification
parameters.description:
modification describes the network policies to add, modify, or delete in
the simulation. Must be non-empty and must not affect restricted namespaces
(stackrox, kube-system).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query filters deployments shown as query-match nodes using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: includePorts
parameters.description: If set to true, include port-level information in the network policy graph.

parameters.name: includeNodeDiff
parameters.description:
include_node_diff controls whether added/removed edge diffs between the
current and simulated graphs are computed and returned. When false, only
the simulated graph and policy list are returned.

parameters.name: scope.query
parameters.description: query filters the scope using StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production").

post.summary:
SendNetworkPolicyYAML delivers the proposed network policy modification
YAML to one or more configured notifiers (e.g. Slack, email).
post.description:
The cluster and all specified notifiers must exist. The modification must
be non-empty. Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if any required field is missing.
Requires Modify access to the Integration resource.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id identifies the target cluster. Required.

parameters.name: modification
parameters.description: modification is the network policy change to send. Must be non-empty.

parameters.name: notifierIds
parameters.description: notifier_ids is the list of notifier IDs to send the YAML to. Required.

get.summary:
GetUndoModificationForDeployment returns the undo record for the most
recent network policy application associated with a specific deployment.
get.description: Returns NOT_FOUND if the deployment does not exist or has no undo record.

get.summary:
GetUndoModification returns the undo record for the most recent network
policy application to a cluster.
get.description:
The undo record contains the original modification and the modification
needed to revert it.

Returns NOT_FOUND if no undo record exists for the cluster.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id identifies the cluster whose undo record to retrieve.

get.summary:
GetDiffFlowsFromUndoModificationForDeployment compares the peers currently
allowed by active NetworkPolicies against those that would be allowed if
the last deployment-level undo modification were applied.
get.description: Returns NOT_FOUND if the deployment has no stored undo record.

get.summary: GetNetworkPolicy returns a single NetworkPolicy by ID.
get.description:
The YAML representation of the policy is populated in the response.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no policy with the given ID exists.

GenerateNetworkPoliciesRequestDeleteExis.description:
- NONE: Do not delete any existing network policies.
 - GENERATED_ONLY: Delete any existing *auto-generated* network policies.
 - ALL: Delete all existing network policies in the respective namespace.

default.description: `default` indicates whether the external source is user-generated or system-generated.

discovered.description: `discovered` indicates whether the external source is harvested from monitored traffic.

NetworkEntityInfoExternalSource.description: Update normalizeDupNameExtSrcs(...) in `central/networkgraph/aggregator/aggregator.go` whenever this message is updated.

modification.description: modification describes the policies to create/update and the policies to delete.

NetworkPolicyServiceApplyNetworkPolicyYa.description:
ApplyNetworkPolicyYamlForDeploymentRequest applies a network policy
modification to the cluster of the specified deployment and records an
undo record keyed by deployment ID.

deleteExisting.description:
delete_existing controls whether any existing baseline-generated policy for
this deployment is included in the modification's delete list.

includePorts.description: include_ports controls whether per-port details are reflected in the generated rules.

NetworkPolicyServiceGetBaselineGenerated.description:
GetBaselineGeneratedPolicyForDeploymentRequest requests auto-generation of
a NetworkPolicy for a specific deployment based on its network baseline.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

matchLabels.description:
This is actually a oneof, but we can't make it one due to backwards
compatibility constraints.

storageLabelSelector.title:
Label selector components are joined with logical AND, see
    https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/

ingress.title:
Whether this connection is an ingress/egress, from the PoV
of the deployment whose baseline this is in

port.title: May be 0 if not applicable (e.g., icmp), and denotes the destination port

storageNetworkBaselineConnectionProperti.title:
NetworkBaselineConnectionProperties represents information about a baseline connection
next available tag: 4

storageNetworkEntityInfoType.title: - INTERNAL_ENTITIES: INTERNAL_ENTITIES is for grouping all internal entities under a single network graph node

modification.description:
modification contains the YAML for newly generated policies and references
to policies that should be deleted, per the delete_existing mode.

v1GenerateNetworkPoliciesResponse.description:
Next available tag: 2
GenerateNetworkPoliciesResponse contains the generated NetworkPolicy YAML
modification ready to be applied or previewed.

allowedPeers.description:
allowed_peers is the list of permitted peer connections, including port,
protocol, and ingress/egress direction for each peer.

v1GetAllowedPeersFromCurrentPolicyForDep.description:
GetAllowedPeersFromCurrentPolicyForDeploymentResponse lists all network
peers that are currently permitted to communicate with a deployment based
on the active NetworkPolicies.

modification.description: modification contains the generated policy YAML and any policies to delete.

v1GetBaselineGeneratedPolicyForDeploymen.description:
GetBaselineGeneratedPolicyForDeploymentResponse contains the generated
NetworkPolicy modification for a specific deployment.

entity.description: entity is the peer network entity.

properties.description: properties are the connection properties (port, protocol, direction) for this peer.

v1GetDiffFlowsGroupedFlow.description:
GetDiffFlowsGroupedFlow groups connection properties for a single peer in
one of the two compared policy states (added or removed).

entity.description: entity is the peer network entity.

added.description: added lists connection properties that would be newly permitted in the new state.

removed.description: removed lists connection properties that would no longer be permitted.

unchanged.description: unchanged lists connection properties permitted in both states.

v1GetDiffFlowsReconciledFlow.description:
GetDiffFlowsReconciledFlow captures the connection-level diff for a single
peer between two network policy states.

added.description: added contains peers that are permitted in the new state but not in the current state.

removed.description: removed contains peers that are permitted in the current state but not in the new state.

reconciled.description: reconciled contains peers present in both states, with per-connection property diffs.

v1GetDiffFlowsResponse.description:
GetDiffFlowsResponse describes the diff between two sets of allowed peers
for a deployment (e.g. current policy vs. baseline-generated policy, or
current policy vs. undo state).

undoRecord.description: undo_record describes the original modification and the change needed to revert it.

v1GetUndoModificationForDeploymentRespon.description:
GetUndoModificationForDeploymentResponse contains the undo record for the
last network policy modification applied via ApplyNetworkPolicyYamlForDeployment.

undoRecord.description:
undo_record describes the original modification and the modification
needed to revert it.

v1GetUndoModificationResponse.description:
GetUndoModificationResponse contains the undo record for the last applied
network policy modification to a cluster.

id.description: id is the unique identifier of the peer entity.

type.description: type is the entity type (DEPLOYMENT, EXTERNAL_SOURCE, INTERNET, etc.).

name.description: name is the display name of the peer entity.

discovered.description:
discovered is true if this entity was automatically discovered (e.g. a
discovered external CIDR block) rather than user-defined.

v1NetworkBaselinePeerEntity.description:
NetworkBaselinePeerEntity identifies a network entity (deployment, external
source, etc.) as a peer in a baseline connection check.

entity.description:
The peer entity of the connection. This can be constructed from the
entity object of the networkgraph API. Only the ID and type are required.

port.description: The port and protocol of the destination of the given connection.

ingress.description:
A boolean representing whether the query is for an ingress or egress
connection. This is defined with respect to the current deployment.
Thus:
  - If the connection in question is in the outEdges of the current deployment,
    this should be false.
  - If it is in the outEdges of the peer deployment, this
    should be true.

v1NetworkBaselineStatusPeer.description:
NetworkBaselineStatusPeer describes a single peer connection to be checked
against a deployment's network baseline.

port.description: port is the destination port of the connection.

protocol.description: protocol is the Layer-4 protocol (TCP, UDP, etc.) of the connection.

lastActiveTimestamp.description: last_active_timestamp is the most recent time this connection was observed.

v1NetworkEdgeProperties.description:
NetworkEdgeProperties describes a single observed connection on a specific
port and protocol.

v1NetworkEdgePropertiesBundle.description:
NetworkEdgePropertiesBundle groups all connection properties observed on a
single directed edge between two network nodes.

epoch.description:
epoch is a monotonically increasing counter that increments whenever the
underlying network policy graph changes. Clients can compare epochs to
detect staleness without re-fetching the full graph.

nodes.description:
nodes is the list of network entities in the graph. Edge indices in
out_edges refer to positions in this list.

v1NetworkGraph.description:
NetworkGraph is the response to GetNetworkGraph, containing a snapshot of
observed network flows within a cluster.

nodeDiffs.description: node_diffs maps a node ID to the connectivity changes for that node.

v1NetworkGraphDiff.description:
NetworkGraphDiff summarizes which edges were added or removed between two
network graph states.

epoch.description:
epoch is a monotonically increasing counter. Clients can poll this to
detect when the graph needs to be refreshed.

v1NetworkGraphEpoch.description:
NetworkGraphEpoch is a version counter for the network policy graph of a
cluster. It increments whenever the underlying network policies change.

query.description: query filters the scope using StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production").

v1NetworkGraphScope.description:
NetworkGraphScope restricts the network graph to a subset of deployments
matching the given search query.

entity.description: entity identifies the network entity (deployment, CIDR block, internet, etc.).

internetAccess.description: internet_access is true if this node has observed flows to/from the internet.

policyIds.description:
policy_ids lists the IDs of NetworkPolicies that select this deployment.
Populated only when include_policies is set in the request.

nonIsolatedIngress.description:
non_isolated_ingress is true when no ingress NetworkPolicy selects this deployment,
meaning all ingress traffic is allowed.

nonIsolatedEgress.description:
non_isolated_egress is true when no egress NetworkPolicy selects this deployment,
meaning all egress traffic is allowed.

queryMatch.description:
query_match is true if this node matched the filter query in the request
(as opposed to appearing only as a neighbor of a matching node).

outEdges.description:
out_edges maps the index of a destination node in the nodes list to the
set of observed connection properties on that edge.

v1NetworkNode.description:
NetworkNode represents a single entity (deployment, external source, or
internet) in the network graph.

policyIds.description: policy_ids lists NetworkPolicy IDs that select this deployment in the new state.

outEdges.description: out_edges maps a destination node ID to the edge properties in the new state.

nonIsolatedIngress.description: non_isolated_ingress is true when no ingress policy selects this node in the new state.

nonIsolatedEgress.description: non_isolated_egress is true when no egress policy selects this node in the new state.

v1NetworkNodeDiff.description:
NetworkNodeDiff captures the change to a single network node's connectivity
between two graph states (e.g. current vs. simulated).

policy.description: policy is the network policy in its simulated state (nil if DELETED).

status.description: status indicates how this policy is affected by the proposed modification.

oldPolicy.description:
old_policy is the current (pre-simulation) version of the policy.
Populated when status is MODIFIED or DELETED.

if status is MODIFIED or DELETED, this contains the previous network policy.

v1NetworkPolicyInSimulation.description:
NetworkPolicyInSimulation represents a single network policy in the context
of a simulation, annotated with whether it is unchanged, added, modified,
or deleted by the proposed modification.

v1NetworkPolicyInSimulationStatus.description:
- UNCHANGED: UNCHANGED means this policy is not affected by the proposed modification.
 - MODIFIED: MODIFIED means this policy is replaced by a new version in the simulation.
 - ADDED: ADDED means this policy does not currently exist and would be created.
 - DELETED: DELETED means this policy would be removed by the modification.

simulatedGraph.description:
simulated_graph is the network connectivity graph after applying the
proposed modification.

policies.description:
policies is the full list of network policies in the simulation with their
change status (UNCHANGED, ADDED, MODIFIED, DELETED).

added.description:
added contains nodes and edges that would be added by the modification.
Only populated when include_node_diff is true and the modification has changes.

removed.description:
removed contains nodes and edges that would be removed by the modification.
Only populated when include_node_diff is true and the modification has changes.

v1SimulateNetworkGraphResponse.description:
SimulateNetworkGraphResponse contains the results of a network policy
simulation, including the simulated graph, the policy states, and optional
edge diffs.
